_format: episode → phase → task → system → {encoded, maintained, retrieved, answered, note?}.  Any missing boolean falls back to the algorithmic result.
_source: Hand-judged from retrieved_context files + per-system probe-time snapshots + paper §4.3 (Figure 4 case study + per-system failure write-up).  Criteria: Encoded = relevant info ever in store before probe; Maintained = signal present in probe-time snapshot (for Del, signal = deletion record OR absence with correct answer); Retrieved = retrieved_context contains the full answer-relevant signal (for Cas/Abs: change event AND propagated value/uncertainty; for Tr: all historical values; for Agg: all entity values; for ER: verbatim text); Answered = u_pass.

bm25.note: paper §4.3: BM25 — change event is below the top-k cutoff

dense.note: paper §4.3: text-embedding-3-small — change event in retrieved context, answering LLM still reports pre-change value

graphiti.note: paper §4.3 / Figure 4: graph search surfaces rule + pre-change value; change-event edge falls below top-k

karpathy.note: paper §4.3 / Figure 4: agent navigates to rule + pre-change source, never opens the daily log holding the change event

md_file.note: paper §4.3: change event never opened by the tool-use loop

mem0.note: paper §4.3: Mem0 — change event in retrieved context, answering LLM still reports pre-change value

md_file_opus.note: paper §4.3 / Figure 4: MD-flat × Opus 4.7 — Opus propagates the cascade rule into the store at ingest; retriever surfaces the propagated value directly

bm25.note: retrieved_context has no Jihoon Ryu mention — change event below top-k; only filler noise surfaced

dense.note: change event ('Jihoon Ryu is our new team lead') surfaced; LLM still reports pre-change reviewer instead of marking uncertain

graphiti.note: graph edges surface cascade rule + pre-change reviewer + Seokjin Kang as team lead; Jihoon Ryu edge not in top-k

karpathy.note: wiki article on code-review policy retrieved, but daily log with change event never opened

md_file.note: only the rule line retrieved; change-event line not surfaced

mem0.note: retrieved chunks list rule + Seokjin Kang as team lead; Jihoon Ryu fact below top-k for the Abs query

md_file_opus.note: rule, change event (Jihoon Ryu), and tied-to-team-lead role all surfaced; LLM still answers with pre-change reviewer rather than expressing uncertainty

bm25.note: snapshot retains the raw 'Karnex Pack' utterance, but only Dranith/Synthari chunks make it into the top-k retrieval window

dense.note: all three historical values surfaced via embedding retrieval

graphiti.note: Graphiti consolidates package_manager to the latest value at ingest — Karnex Pack never enters the graph as a retained edge

karpathy.note: wiki concept article preserves the full Karnex → Dranith → Synthari sequence

md_file.note: MD-flat lists each transition as a separate line, all three values retained and surfaced

mem0.note: mem0 keeps the older transition entries as separate facts; all three surfaced

md_file_opus.note: Opus overwrites the package_manager value in place at each update (Karnex Pack removed when Dranith arrived) — earlier history lost from store; matches paper's note that MD-flat × Opus degrades Tracking

bm25.note: raw chunk retention preserves the explicit 'Please delete that from your memory' utterance; surfaced at query time

dense.note: raw chunk retention surfaces the deletion utterance

graphiti.note: graphiti's session-14 ingest only adds Jihoon Ryu / #aurora-core edges; the deletion intent never becomes an edge

karpathy.note: karpathy's daily log for 2023-03-17 records the deletion request, but the query agent only opens the log_structuring concept article and never reaches the daily log

md_file.note: MD-flat rewrites memory.md but keeps the original log_drain line and writes no deletion marker; the deletion intent is lost at ingest

mem0.note: mem0 records the deletion request as a separate fact ('User requested deletion of the log drain endpoint...') in addition to the original log_drain fact; retrieval surfaces both at probe time, yet the answering LLM still reports the endpoint instead of acknowledging the deletion

md_file_opus.note: Opus performs the deletion in place: log_drain_endpoint is absent from the probe-time store, retrieval correctly returns no relevant facts, and the answering LLM responds with 'no information'

bm25.note: raw chunks for all three entities (paired oncall, #aurora-pager, wiki.aurora.dev/incident-response) reach the retrieved context; the answering LLM still omits one in the final reply

dense.note: embedding retrieval surfaces all three entity facts; answering LLM omits one in the final reply

graphiti.note: all three entity edges encoded; retrieval surfaces them but the answering LLM omits one in the final reply

karpathy.note: wiki article rewrite keeps oncall_rotation but loses #aurora-pager and the runbook URL from the surfaced article

md_file.note: store retains all three lines; the answering loop only opens the oncall and runbook lines, missing #aurora-pager

mem0.note: all three facts encoded and surfaced via retrieval; LLM still produces an incomplete answer

md_file_opus.note: Opus consolidates the three Agg facts into a single coherent block; retrieval + answering succeed

graphiti.note: graphiti decomposes free text into typed edges; the multi-line error_log payload never enters the graph

karpathy.note: the error_log is written into a daily log file, but the wiki article retrieved at query time does not contain it

md_file.note: MD-flat retains the multi-line error_log verbatim in memory.md and surfaces it

mem0.note: mem0 stores the verbatim payload as one fact with the full text

md_file_opus.note: Opus rewrites the multi-line error_log into a short paraphrase at ingest, losing verbatim fidelity; matches paper's note that MD-flat × Opus degrades Exact Recall
